refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify that energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce idle and unnecessary movements
Monitor and report energy usage and tonnage moved

selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of energy-efficient powertrain design, intelligent task scheduling algorithm, and real-time energy monitoring system

potential_sources: Energy monitoring data feeding task scheduling optimization
Powertrain efficiency impacting energy consumption metrics
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but designed to minimize cross-domain dependencies by modularizing monitoring and control.

assumptions_risks: Accurate energy and tonnage measurement sensors are available and reliable
Task scheduling algorithms can be integrated with vehicle control systems
Powertrain improvements do not compromise safety or operational constraints
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Further refine task scheduling DP to explicitly include interface requirements with teleoperation and safety services.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components responsible for powertrain and energy consumption

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends real-time energy usage and operational data

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives optimized task schedules from Teleoperation or NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges safety status and alerts with Safety Services

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of powertrain and energy consumption functions; low diffusion risk.
Contains task scheduling logic but limited powertrain control.
Primarily safety monitoring; minimal energy efficiency functions.
Coordinates overall system but less direct containment of powertrain.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning energy efficiency DPs related to powertrain and energy monitoring to Vehicle module maximizes containment and minimizes diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap of task scheduling logic between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Energy monitoring data interface complexity across modules
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify boundaries and interfaces for task scheduling between Vehicle and Teleoperation to reduce diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well-defined and measurable; selected DPs logically satisfy the FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Task scheduling logic diffusion between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for powertrain and energy monitoring; task scheduling requires clearer interface definition.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and operational logs; interfaces support data collection and control.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of DPs to Vehicle module is clear for powertrain and monitoring; task scheduling allocation needs refinement.

recommended_revisions: Refine task scheduling DP and IM boundaries to reduce diffusion between Vehicle and Teleoperation.
Define explicit interface protocols for energy monitoring data exchange.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are mostly sound, diffusion risks in task scheduling and energy monitoring interfaces require boundary clarifications before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce idle and unnecessary movements
Monitor and report energy usage and tonnage moved

revise.why: To clarify task scheduling DP and its IM assignment, and to define explicit interfaces to reduce diffusion risks.

assumptions: Reliable sensors and telemetry infrastructure exist
Task scheduling can be modularized and interfaced cleanly
open_risks: Potential interface complexity causing integration delays
Safety criticality may impose constraints on task scheduling flexibility
evidence_needed: Interface specifications between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Telemetry data accuracy and latency measurements
